Model Module¶

class fivedreg.model.ModelHandler(hidden_layers: List[int] = [64, 32, 16], learning_rate: float = 0.001, max_epochs: int = 100)[source]¶

Bases: object

fit(X: ndarray, y: ndarray, verbose: bool = False)[source]¶

Trains the model using Scikit-Learn MLPRegressor.

predict(X: ndarray) → ndarray[source]¶

Predicts using the trained model.

ModelHandler Class¶

class fivedreg.model.ModelHandler(hidden_layers: List[int] = [64, 32, 16], learning_rate: float = 0.001, max_epochs: int = 100)[source]¶

Bases: object

__init__(hidden_layers: List[int] = [64, 32, 16], learning_rate: float = 0.001, max_epochs: int = 100)[source]¶
fit(X: ndarray, y: ndarray, verbose: bool = False)[source]¶

Trains the model using Scikit-Learn MLPRegressor.

predict(X: ndarray) → ndarray[source]¶

Predicts using the trained model.

fit(X: ndarray, y: ndarray, verbose: bool = False)[source]¶

Trains the model using Scikit-Learn MLPRegressor.

predict(X: ndarray) → ndarray[source]¶

Predicts using the trained model.

Usage Example¶

from fivedreg.model import ModelHandler
import numpy as np

# Initialize model with custom config
model = ModelHandler(
    hidden_layers=[128, 64, 32],
    learning_rate=0.0005,
    max_epochs=150
)

# Train
X_train = np.random.rand(1000, 5)
y_train = np.random.rand(1000)

results = model.fit(X_train, y_train)
print(f"Training time: {results['training_time']:.4f}s")
print(f"Final loss: {results['losses'][-1]:.6f}")

# Predict
X_new = np.random.rand(10, 5)
predictions = model.predict(X_new)
print(f"Predictions: {predictions}")
